questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some boys are girls. All girls are cute. Conclusions: I. Some boys are cute. II. Some cute are girls. III. Some cute are girls. IV. No cute are girls.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some copies are desks. No desks are pens. Conclusions: I. Some copies are pens. II. Some copies are not pens. III. Some pens are desks. IV. Some pens are not desks.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All books are pens. Some books are pencils. Conclusions: I. Some pens are books. II. No pens are books. III. Some books are pencils. IV. Some books are not pencils.

choices.d: Either I or II and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: No systems are desks. All desks are books. Conclusions: I. Some systems are books. II. Some systems are not books. III. Some books are systems. IV. Some books are not systems.

choices.d: I and either III or IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some singers are rockers. All rockers are westerners. Conclusions: I. Some rockers are singers. II. Some westerners are rockers. III. Some singers are westerners. IV. Some singers are not westerners.

choices.a: Either I or II and either III or IV follow
choices.c: Either III or IV and I follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: III and IV follow. III and IV follow. All cats are bulls. Conclusions: I. Some bulls are dogs. II. Some cats are dogs. III. No cats are dogs. IV. Some bulls are cats.

choices.d: IV and either II or III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All bars are books. Some bars are books. Conclusions: I. Some books are bars. II. Some books are coins. III. Some books are not bars. IV. Some books are not coins.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some birds are elephants. All stars are not elephants. Conclusions: I. All stars are elephants. II. All stars are elephants. III. Some elephants are not stars. IV. All elephants are stars.

choices.a: Either I or II and either III or IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: No book is a book. All books are birds. Conclusions: I. Some birds are not books. II. Some birds are books. III. Some books are not birds. IV. Some books are birds.

choices.a: Either I or II and either III or IV follow
choices.b: Either I or II and III follow
choices.c: Either III or IV and I follow
